name: Autoimmune Polyendocrine Syndrome Type 1
mechanismChain: AIRE loss impairs thymic expression of tissue-restricted self antigens.
Autoreactive T cells escape central tolerance deletion.
Escaped clones seed peripheral tissues and recruit inflammatory effectors.
Multigland autoimmunity follows with chronic endocrine failure.

name: IPEX Syndrome
mechanismChain: FOXP3 defects cripple regulatory T cell lineage programming.
Suppressive control of activated effector T cells fails.
Peripheral checkpoint restraint collapses across gut, skin, and endocrine tissues.
Early severe autoimmunity emerges with enteropathy and endocrinopathy.

name: CTLA4 Haploinsufficiency
mechanismChain: Reduced CTLA4 dosage weakens inhibitory signaling on activated T cells.
Persistent costimulation amplifies effector responses and tissue infiltration.
B-cell help becomes excessive and dysregulated.
Combined autoimmunity with hypogammaglobulinemia-like features develops.

name: Autoimmune Lymphoproliferative Syndrome (FAS)
mechanismChain: FAS signaling defects block activation-induced apoptosis in lymphocytes.
Autoreactive and overactivated clones persist after immune responses.
Chronic lymphoproliferation sustains aberrant immune activation.
Autoimmune cytopenias and organomegaly follow from failed contraction.

name: A20 Haploinsufficiency
mechanismChain: TNFAIP3 loss reduces A20-mediated negative feedback on NF-κB signaling.
Inflammatory transcription persists after cytokine or receptor stimulation.
Innate and adaptive immune circuits remain in a primed inflammatory state.
Behçet-like and autoimmune phenotypes emerge from unchecked inflammatory tone.

name: TREX1-related Interferonopathy
mechanismChain: TREX1 dysfunction allows intracellular DNA debris to accumulate.
Nucleic acid sensing pathways misclassify self DNA as viral danger.
Chronic type I interferon production establishes sustained antiviral tone.
Interferon-driven inflammation produces lupus-like autoimmunity and tissue injury.

name: STAT3 Gain-of-Function Disease
mechanismChain: Activating STAT3 variants prolong cytokine-driven transcriptional responses.
Effector differentiation outweighs regulatory counterprograms.
Barrier and endocrine tissues face chronic immune attack.
Multisystem autoimmunity coexists with recurrent infections and growth failure.

name: C1q Deficiency
mechanismChain: Absent C1q impairs clearance of apoptotic debris and immune complexes.
Persistent self-antigen complexes remain available to antigen-presenting cells.
Autoantibody responses intensify and deposit immune complexes in tissues.
Severe early lupus-like disease develops with complement-mediated injury.

name: TACI-related CVID with Autoimmunity
mechanismChain: TNFRSF13B variants alter B-cell survival and class-switch checkpoints.
Low-affinity or autoreactive clones persist in peripheral B-cell compartments.
Immunoglobulin quality and quantity become unstable.
Recurrent infections coexist with autoimmune cytopenias and organ autoimmunity.
